enrichment.summary: Kyle hosts the first episode of 'News with Kyle' at 1:30 AM while high, scrolling through the Huffington Post and riffing on current stories. He covers US Middle East policy, Russia's role in Syria, the 2016 Republican primary, the Supreme Court vacancy, North Korea, Greece's pension crisis, and Pope Francis criticizing Mexico's wealthy elite, weaving in broader takes on capitalism vs. socialism and populism.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: US involvement in the Middle East
stances.position: should stay out entirely; Russia has historical and geographic claim to that sphere
Russia wants peace there; let them handle it instead of spending US money
won't last more than five years due to internal instability
too brash, will make bad conservative appointments, Republicans are scared of him
his tax-the-rich policies will create a more meritocratic, competitive society
dying because it appeals to people and power structures that no longer exist
Obama must nominate; Rubio's pledge to block is a direct violation of checks and balances
socialism is better because people can vote out bad decision-makers; corporations cannot be voted out

stances.topic: Pope Francis criticizing Mexico's wealthy elite
stances.position: correct; wealth inequality must be addressed by investing in people
should be government-run so citizens can vote out bad management

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm really excited super super high right now, and it's one thirty in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to call and see if one of my Adobe programs can do the thing where you see a little mirror window of my face while I record.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I read posts and then talk about what I listened to during the day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to remember to do it quick because the stories go away.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to write the stories down so I don't forget them.

claims.subject: cyber tech plan
claims.claim: The cyber tech plan is about shutting down enemy silos and making them spin out of control.

claims.subject: Middle East
claims.claim: We need to stay the fuck out of the Middle East because it's Russia's business since they're closer.

claims.subject: Turkey
claims.claim: Turkey was the Byzantine Empire, and when it fell, the Zars moved to Russia and tried to reignite the Byzantine Empire.

claims.subject: Russia
claims.claim: Russia is like a child of the Middle East because of its historical ties to the Byzantine Empire.

claims.subject: America
claims.claim: America caused problems in the Middle East by invading Iraq and should be banned from interfering there.

claims.subject: Russia
claims.claim: Russia wants peace in the Middle East, but they do fucked-up things too.

claims.subject: Republican Party
claims.claim: The Republican Party and warmongers are lying to each other and calling each other pieces of shit in debates.

claims.subject: North Korea
claims.claim: North Korea is so backwards in the modern world that its leader won't last more than five years.

claims.subject: North Korea
claims.claim: North Korea could get invaded easily because they have no real power.

claims.subject: Indians
claims.claim: Indians are starting to upload a lot of videos on YouTube, and soon there will be way more Indian content than American.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Everybody hates Donald Trump because he's too brash and will piss off too many people.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Donald Trump will make bad political decisions and put stupid conservative people in power.

claims.subject: Republican Party
claims.claim: The Republican Party is scared because this might be their last chance to win an election.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders is going to tax the rich heavily and fund a lot of social programs.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders will make the world more meritocratic by giving people resources to compete.

claims.subject: Republican Party
claims.claim: The Republican Party is appealing to ignorant people who don't realize how outdated their views are.

claims.subject: Ted Cruz
claims.claim: Ted Cruz got mad about a porn star being in his ad, showing how out of touch he is.

claims.subject: women
claims.claim: Women are getting more comfortable showing their bodies, and soon they'll be fully nude in public.

claims.subject: Pope Francis
claims.claim: Pope Francis criticized Mexico's rich elite for the wealth disparity and corruption.

claims.subject: cell phones
claims.claim: Cell phone services should be government-run so people can vote out the controllers if they do a bad job.

claims.subject: Greece
claims.claim: Greece's seniors are getting only a quarter of their pensions, and their kids are unemployed.

claims.subject: Greece
claims.claim: Unemployed Greeks should be doing something useful instead of just sitting around.

claims.subject: Obama
claims.claim: Obama is urging Russia to stop bombing Syrian rebels, but we should stay out of it.

claims.subject: Supreme Court
claims.claim: The president has the constitutional right to nominate a Supreme Court justice, and the Senate should review it, not just block it.

claims.subject: Marco Rubio
claims.claim: Marco Rubio saying he'll block any Supreme Court nomination is undemocratic and violates checks and balances.

claims.subject: Supreme Court
claims.claim: The next Supreme Court nomination is crucial because it will break the tie between liberal and conservative justices.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: If Trump wins, he'll nominate a bad Supreme Court justice, but if Sanders wins, it'll be a good one.
